codeReview.summary: The implementation delivers the core chrome cleanup requested by VAL-VISUAL-033: `GraphShell` removes the persistent minimap, focused-surface minimap, investor-path progress bar, and sticky inspector dock, leaving the overview bar, node grid, and depth-on-demand surface as the dominant flow. I found one non-blocking fidelity issue: the investor-path affordance still adds a second stacked guidance row ahead of the grid, so the default workspace is not quite reduced to only one clean top-nav + graph grid yet.

issues.description: `InvestorPathAffordance` still renders the static `8 gates Â· problem â†’ wedge â†’ proof â†’ moat â†’ business model â†’ roadmap â†’ valuation â†’ evidence` summary directly beneath the orientation bar. The major chrome layers are gone, but this leftover guidance row still competes with the 'single lightweight orientation mechanism' / 'one clean top nav + graph node grid' goal, so the workspace is not fully as pared back as the feature description calls for.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `frontend-experience-worker` still points shell/navigation work toward minimap + sticky-inspector chrome that the current visual direction has explicitly removed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:31` says shell work should prefer a graph workspace with `minimap/orientation controls, and a sticky inspector`, but mission guidance now says to simplify graph chrome to one clean navigation model and remove redundant controls (`/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/AGENTS.md:182-185`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture library still documents sticky-inspector/minimap chrome as the preferred graph-shell pattern, so future workers could reintroduce the layers this feature just removed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/architecture.md:25` recommends a graph workspace with `sticky inspector, minimap, and explicit camera/orientation state`, which now conflicts with the mission's graph-chrome simplification guidance in `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/AGENTS.md:182-185`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill-adherence record is not fully auditable for this shell change: the skill requires a design-system review before shell work, but the saved transcript does not show that read even though the handoff reports `followedProcedure=true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md:22` requires reading `.factory/library/design-system.md` for shell changes. String checks against the transcript skeleton for worker session `41e2400c-e310-4807-b31d-83263870eafe` found no `Read` of that file, while the handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` with no deviations in `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/handoffs/2026-03-23T14-21-49-426Z__redesign-clean-graph-workspace-and-information-flow__41e2400c-e310-4807-b31d-83263870eafe.json:120-121`.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking issue. The feature removes the requested chrome layers and restores a much cleaner graph-first workspace, but the investor-path affordance still leaves a secondary guidance row ahead of the grid. Shared-state follow-up: the frontend skill and architecture library still describe minimap/sticky-inspector patterns that this mission direction now rejects, and the recorded skill-adherence evidence for required design-system review is incomplete.
